item.title: Photos of the Week: Screeching Championship, Direct Democracy, Victory Plunge
item.description: Parkour practice in the mountains of Afghanistan, an Anzac Day parade in Australia, a lunar-landing art project in Northern Ireland, corgi racing in the Czech Republic, and much more
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: A New World Record
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: So, About That AI Bubble
item.description: Thanks to the rise of Claude Code and other AI agents, revenues are finally catching up to the hype.
item.dc:creator: Rogé Karma

item.title: Iran’s Leaders Mostly Want a Deal
item.description: Sure, there’s “infighting,” but not along the lines many assume.
item.dc:creator: Arash Azizi

item.title: How You Felt About Obama Saying ‘If I Had a Son’ Explains Everything
item.description: Would he look like Trayvon Martin?
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: The Secret to Success Is ‘Monotasking’
item.description: In a world full of distractions, getting your brain to focus on one thing at a time requires radical measures.
item.dc:creator: David Epstein

item.title: The Trump Administration Casts Out the ‘Soul’ of MAHA
item.description: The withdrawal of Casey Means’s nomination for surgeon general is the latest blow to the movement.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bartlett

item.title: Vance Denies and ConfirmsAtlanticReporting in One Breath
item.description: The vice president’s comments on Fox News are the latest instance of his tortured attempts to navigate a path through Donald Trump’s war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Seven Death-Defying Books for the Adventurous Reader
item.description: These titles will spirit you to some of the planet’s wildest landscapes, without making you leave your armchair.
item.dc:creator: Eva Holland

item.title: Making America’s Houses Bigger May Have Been a Mistake
item.description: Millennials are abandoning the idea of living in a giant home.
item.dc:creator: Lily Meyer

item.title: The Iran War’s Ramifications Have Only Just Begun
item.description: U.S. goals haven’t been met, but the war will cause long-term disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Nancy A. Youssef

item.title: All the Sad Young Chinese Professionals
item.description: China’s urbanites are learning the price of prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Michael Schuman

item.title: The ‘Great Man’ Presidency
item.description: Is Donald Trump reading Hegel?
item.dc:creator: Hanna Rosin

item.title: The Era of Normie Extremism Is Here
item.description: What the alleged Washington hotel attacker has in common with Luigi Mangione
item.dc:creator: Ali Breland

item.title: The Secret Weapon Against AI Dominance
item.description: The future of creative labor will turn on whether AI-generated work can be copyrighted.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Noti-Victor

item.title: Child Care Is Buckling
item.description: The president’s broad policies are making a bad situation worse.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Haspel

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: ‘Great Men’ and Cataclysmic Events
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Micah Lasher, Child Magician
item.description: The race for New York’s Twelfth District keeps getting more interesting.
item.dc:creator: Joel Stein

item.title: This ABC Showdown Is Different
item.description: The network is now in a stronger position to defend itself against the FCC.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: Voters Can Be Disenfranchised Now
item.description: Just say it’s because they’re Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer
